title: Houses for rent in Mentor, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Mentor, OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in Mentor, Ohio: rent benchmarks, how Section 8 vouchers work outside Cuyahoga County, and Ohio tenant law. Tell our team what you need.
answerBox: Mentor is Lake County's largest city, about 25 miles northeast of Cleveland via I-90, near Headlands Beach State Park. Rent Finder Cleveland's 90+ rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland and a few nearby Ohio cities, and we don't currently manage a property inside Mentor — but we can tell you about comparable voucher-friendly homes and how vouchers work outside Cuyahoga County.

body: Mentor is a Lake County city roughly 25 miles northeast of Cleveland along the I-90 corridor, and it's the largest city in Lake County. It sits on Lake Erie, near Headlands Beach State Park, and includes the Great Lakes Mall commercial corridor along Route 615/Mentor Avenue.

Housing in Mentor spans postwar single-family subdivisions closer to the lake to newer construction further from the shoreline, which is typical of Lake County's suburban growth pattern since the mid-20th century.

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses for rent in Mentor?
body: Not currently. Our portfolio of 90+ rental homes is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — with a smaller west-side presence and a few homes further out in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio. We don't have a listing inside Mentor at this time.

If Mentor is where you need to be, [reach out to our leasing team](/p/book-showing) and we'll tell you about comparable voucher-friendly homes closer to Cleveland, or you can browse the [Cleveland houses pillar](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) and the [Cleveland suburbs guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) for other areas we currently serve.

sections.h2: How Section 8 vouchers work if you're searching in Mentor
body: Mentor is in Lake County, not Cuyahoga County, so the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) — the agency that administers vouchers for Cleveland and Cuyahoga County — is not the agency covering Mentor. Housing Choice Vouchers nationwide are run locally by the public housing authority (PHA) with jurisdiction over a given address; HUD's [Housing Choice Voucher program overview](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers) explains how the program works and can help you identify the correct PHA for a Lake County address.

Ohio has no statewide law requiring a landlord to accept a voucher as a source of income, and no local ordinance covering Mentor was identified in this review — confirm directly with the city and with a landlord before assuming voucher acceptance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready; see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how that works for our own portfolio.

sections.h2: What houses cost to rent near Greater Cleveland
body: There's no Mentor-specific rent index available, but two dated reports on the broader Cleveland metro area are a useful reference point. Per Zumper, dated July 4, 2026, the metro's median asking rent is $1,250/month. Per RentCafe (Yardi), dated July 2, 2026, the average rent across professionally managed apartments is $1,564/month for about 787 square feet — a gap that reflects RentCafe's focus on larger managed complexes versus Zumper's broader listing median.

sections.h2: Ohio landlord-tenant law that applies in Mentor
body: Ohio's landlord-tenant statute is state law and applies in Mentor the same as anywhere else in Ohio. Under [ORC 5321.16](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-5321.16), a security deposit over $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) earns 5% annual interest once a tenant stays six months or more, and must be returned with an itemized statement within 30 days of move-out. Before an eviction filing for nonpayment, a landlord must serve a statutory 3-day notice under [ORC 1923.04](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-1923.04). This is general information, not legal advice — verify current statute language before relying on it.

We don't have a Mentor listing today, but tell us what you're looking for and we'll flag comparable voucher-friendly homes closer to Cleveland.

faq.q: Does CMHA cover Section 8 vouchers in Mentor, Ohio?
faq.a: No. CMHA administers vouchers for Cuyahoga County, and Mentor is in Lake County. Housing Choice Vouchers are run locally by the public housing authority with jurisdiction over your address — use HUD's program overview to find the correct agency for a Lake County address.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Mentor, Ohio?
faq.a: There's no Mentor-specific index, but the wider Cleveland metro's median asking rent was $1,250/month per Zumper (July 4, 2026), and RentCafe's average across managed apartments was $1,564/month (July 2, 2026). Local Mentor rents will vary by home size and condition.

faq.q: Is Section 8 protected by law in Mentor, Ohio?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and no local ordinance covering Mentor was identified in this review. Acceptance depends on the individual landlord, so confirm directly before applying and check current city rules.

faq.q: What Ohio law covers security deposits in Mentor?
faq.a: Ohio Revised Code 5321.16 applies statewide, including Mentor. Deposits exceeding $50 or one month's rent earn 5% annual interest after six months of tenancy, and landlords must return deposits with an itemized statement within 30 days of move-out. This is general information, not legal advice.
